steps.title: Identify the key signal
steps.content: The passage describes how past and future concepts point in opposite directions along imagined axes (the linear-axis pattern, exemplified by English), then states Núñez and colleagues claim this is “not the case”—meaning not universal across all cultures.

steps.title: Apply the signal to the choices
steps.content: Choice D demonstrates that Yupno future and past gestures don't point in opposite directions, meaning they lack the linear-axis organization. The other choices address hand preference, linguistic overlap, and gesture location—details unrelated to whether temporal concepts follow this directional pattern. This evidence directly shows the Yupno don't follow the universal linear-axis tendency, supporting Núñez and colleagues' claim. That matches choice D — D is correct.
